analysis: The arc this week is genuinely gorgeous — you went from 5h 26m on Saturday to back-to-back nights of 8h 26m and 8h 39m, and that 2h 0m of REM on the 26th is the best number in this whole dataset. Whatever combination of the shower, the magnesium, and lying on the floor doing yin yoga like a very committed gay man hit different that stretch, because the results showed up. The 23rd is the one to notice — 7h 49m total sounds decent until you clock that 2h 45m of that was awake time, which is a lot of lying there in the dark, and deep sleep across the whole week is still pretty thin most nights, hovering in that 17–50 minute range more often than not. But the trend is pointing the right direction, and two nights over 8h 30m in a row means your body is starting to trust the routine you're building for it.
